narrative.headline: What 2017 Jeep Cherokee owners report most
narrative.summary: Powertrain complaints dominate owner reports for this model, with transmission behavior being the most common concern. Electrical system failures, including unexpected shutdowns while driving, make up the next largest share of complaints, with some incidents involving fires or crashes. Engine and speed control issues round out the picture, with owners describing stop/start system interruptions and unintended acceleration during cruise control use.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report that the nine-speed transmission fails to engage its highest gear under any driving conditions, with dealers acknowledging the behavior but describing it as normal. · 5 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle completely and randomly shutting down while in motion, with some incidents occurring on vehicles with very low mileage within months of purchase. · 3 fire · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report that the stop/start feature cuts engine power at critical moments, such as mid-turn when pulling into traffic, interrupting acceleration when it is needed most. · 2 fire · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report that while cruise control is active, the vehicle independently accelerates and the engine revs without driver input, returning to normal speed only after brakes are applied. · 2 crash · 2 injured
